channel.title: Posts on Siwon Choi
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Siwon Choi

item.title: Freezing hidden states across languages: English vs Korean in Llama-3.2-1B
item.description:
Attend First, Consolidate Later: On the Importance of Attention in Different LLM Layers

Code: add english vs korean freeze comparison and clean dataset

Continuing on the last post, I tried to check if the model behaves differently when the prompt is given in Korean. To isolate the effect of processing Korean from the effect of producing Korean tokens, I held the answer (the capital city for each country) fixed as a single English token and varied only the prompt language.

item.title: Reproducing Attend-First-Consolidate-Later Paper: Freezing
item.description:
Attend First, Consolidate Later: On the Importance of Attention in Different LLM Layers

The claim I tested

This paper suggests that LLMs have a two-phase processing where the first part gathers informations, and another part consolidates that information. To support this claim, they consider manipulations such as inserting noise in hidden states, or freezing them. In this post, I test the claim that freezing the model at layer k and copying the hidden state at that layer to all continuing layers does not lead to significant performance drop.
